name: 40 in Focus: SummerStage through the Lens of Photographer Jack Vartoogian
description: From 1987 to 2019, celebrated NYC photographer Jack Vartoogian captured hundreds of SummerStage performances across Manhattan parks, from Central Park to Marcus Garvey Park and Tompkins Square Park. This selection of intimate, rarely seen imagery gives viewers a look back at past decades of outdoor live music in New York City, a peek behind the curtain with never-before-seen backstage photos and honors those artists who have passed on. From the earliest performances with Sun Ra Arkestra and Mavis and Pop Staples, to legendary artists in memoriam like Tito Puente and Hugh Masekela and backstage shots of Buddy Guy and Darlene Love, this exhibition celebrates not only a photographer's commitment to documenting performance but also celebrates a 40-year legacy of SummerStage. This free exhibition is presented by City Parks Foundation in collaboration with NYC Parks Art & Antiquities department. An opening reception will be held on June 16 from 6pm-8 pm, ...

name: Zumba Gold®
description: Zumba Gold® offers a low-impact workout set to a fusion of Latin, International and popular music dance themes, creating a dynamic, exciting, effective fitness system! The routines feature aerobic fitness interval training with a combination of fast and slow rhythms that challenge both the heart and muscles. With fun music and easy-to-follow steps, you can't go wrong! Please register for updates about cancellations. Class is first come, first served, but for safety reasons, participants will not be allowed to join after the first fifteen minutes of class. New Settlement Community Center requires all first-time participants to create a profile with the site upon arrival so please arrive at least 15 minutes in advance.

description: Le Carrousel Extravaganza is a lively, all-in-one entertainment experience featuring magic, puppetry, science, music, and storytelling. Designed to engage children of all ages, this dynamic program sparks imagination, encourages curiosity, and delivers fun-filled, interactive performances for every audience. name: Monday Morning Pitch In to Pick Up
description: Volunteer with us! We will be cleaning up litter to prevent it from entering the Bronx River. Your efforts not only protect wildlife & the rest of the Bronx River ecosystem, but they also help us identify the sources of trash so we can stop the pollution where it starts! We will provide gloves and tools for collecting the trash!
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

name: NYC Public Beaches
description:
Parks maintains 14 miles of beaches, which open Memorial Day weekend. During beach season, lifeguards are on duty daily, from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. Swimming is prohibited when lifeguards are not on duty and in closed sections. Closed sections are marked with signs and/or red flags. 
 
 Sign up for Notify NYC and opt-in to "beach notifications" to receive real-time opening and closing notifications. 
 Photo credit: Benny Polatseck/Mayoral Photography Office

name: Session 2: Parent & Tots Learn to Swim Liberty Pool (Class A)
description: The Learn to Swim program puts the fun in fundamentals and teaches basic swimming and safety skills. The program offers tots, from 1 1\/2 to 5 years old, the opportunity to become comfortable in the water and prepare to learn to swim. Parents\/Guardians must accompany tots in the water. Registration for this program takes place by lottery drawing. Visit https:\/\/nyc.gov\/parks\/learntoswim for rules and regulations.
address: Main Pool in Detective Keith L Williams Park

name: Summer on the Hudson: Art in the Park
description: Art in the Park invites children ages 3-8 to learn about their local environment through a mix of arts & crafts activities. This hands-on, place-based curriculum builds kids' appreciation for the plants, pollinators, and wildlife found in Riverside Park, and fosters their creativity. Mondays and Thursdays at Camel Playground ; Tuesdays and Wednesdays at Hippo Playground . Art in the Park is fully funded by proceeds from the Hippo Playground Spring Fair.

name: Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Yoga with Nicole, a class to be enjoyed by all. Experience a fun and light-hearted, mid-intensity flow that connects breath to movement as you flow through poses, with modifications for everyone. Class will focus on mobility and stability to ensure you leave feeling challenged, balanced, and relaxed. Please bring a mat! Please register for class updates. Class will be held in the Community Garden next to Gerritsen Beach Library.

name: Senior Cardio Sculpt
description: This low impact cardio workout consists of functional movement exercises designed to make daily activities easier to perform. Participants must be 60+ to attend this class. The site requires a separate form for the Department of Aging for all participants. Please come earlier to fill out the consent form.

name: Step Aerobics
description: Step Aerobics is a cardio workout that will elevate your heart rate, strengthen muscles, and improve coordination and balance. Please register for updates about cancellations. Class is first come, first served, but for safety reasons, participants will not be allowed to join after the first fifteen minutes of class. New Settlement Community Center requires all first-time participants to create a profile with the site upon arrival so please arrive at least 15 minutes in advance.

name: Ongoing Outdoor Museum Exhibit: Alice's Terracotta Garden
description: Terra cotta sculpture garden featuring antique architectural ornaments from Tottenville's Atlantic Terra Cotta Company (west side lawn of Biddle House). NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States.
address: H.H. Biddle House in Conference House Park

name: Reel Talks at Bryant Park: Conversation on Eric Rohmer
description: Enjoy a conversation on film director Eric Rohmer with André Aciman, hosted by film connoisseur Scott Adlerberg. André Aciman is the New York Times bestselling author of Call Me by Your Name, Out of Egypt, Eight White Nights, False Papers, Alibis, Harvard Square, Enigma Variations, Homo Irrealis, Roman Year, and Find Me. His most recent book is Room on the Sea (2025). He's the editor of The Proust Project and teaches comparative literature at the Graduate Center of the City University of New York. He lives with his wife in Manhattan. Scott Adlerberg is the author of five novels, including Graveyard Love, Jack Waters, and The Screaming Child. He has written many short stories and contributes pieces regularly to sites such as CrimeReads and Mystery Tribune. He was born in New York City and lives in Brooklyn.

name: Pétanque in the Park
description: Never heard of Pétanque? Our expert friends at NYC's oldest running Pétanque club will teach you. This French lawn bowling game from the early 20th century is easy to learn, and we provide the boules. Just bring yourself!

name: Bryant Park Movie Nights
description: Movie Nights at Bryant Park returns for its 33rd season! Bring a blanket and a friend and grab your spot on the lawn to watch a feature film on the big screen under the stars. The lawn opens at 5pm and films begin at 8pm. Food items for sale curated by Hester Street Fair are available 4pm-8:30pm on the Fountain Terrace, and beer and wine can be purchased on the western end of the lawn beginning at 5pm. Paramount+ Movie Nights at Bryant Park are supported by Time Out and Sour Patch Kids. Jul 13: Wayne's World Jul 20: Good Morning Vietnam Jul 27: The Truman Show Aug 03: Bridget Jones's Diary Aug 10: Catch Me If You Can Aug 17: City of God Aug 24: King Creole Aug 31: No Country for Old Men Sep 07: Galaxy Quest Sept 14: Shakespeare in Love
address: Bryant Park Lawn in Bryant Park

name: Chair Yoga
description: This uplifting seated flow is designed to get you moving and feeling energized-without stressing your joints. Using gentle, rhythmic movements, we'll raise your heart rate, improve balance, and build strength. Perfect for all fitness levels, especially those seeking a low-impact workout that supports mobility and confidence.

name: Bodyweight Circuit Training
description: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full body workout. Whether you are a beginner or looking for a challenge; this class for all fitness levels and modifications are always provided. Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups, get your heart pumping, and connect with people in your community!
address: MVMNT Fitness and Health Studio in Prospect Park

name: Bodyweight Interval Training
description: Bodyweight Interval Training is an energetic yet low impact exercise class that will get your muscles moving! This mix of cardio and body weight strength exercises is a great way to get a full body workout. Whether you're new to exercise or not, the modifications offered in this class make it open to all fitness levels. Come check it out!\u202FPlease bring your own yoga mat!
location_name: Multi-Use Room C in McCarren Play Center
address: Multi-Use Room C in McCarren Play Center

name: Big City Folk in Greeley Square Park
description: See New York City's finest singer-songwriters showcase their newest work, presented by Big City Folk and hosted by songsmith Niall Connolly. With a list of past featured artists including Lana Del Ray, Anais Mitchel and Lucius, it may even be an opportunity to see the next big star in action.

name: Cardio Dance
description: If cardio is cardi-NO for you, give this Cardio Dance class a try! Come enjoy the beats of Latin rhythms paired with choreography that will not only provide a great workout- but a lot of fun as well! This class is open to all levels.

name: Hoop Jam in Herald Square Park
description: We're collaborating with Big Apple Hooping to bring hooping fun to Herald Square Park. If you're looking to learn the fundamentals of hooping, work on refining your tricks, or meet fellow hoopers from the NYC area, this is the event for you. Hoopers of all experience levels, from beginner to advanced, are welcome, and all equipment is provided. No registration is required, just stop by and join us!

name: Low Impact Bodyweight Circuit Training
description: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full body workout. Whether you are a beginner or looking for a challenge- this class for you! Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups and get your heart pumping!
location_name: Multi-Use Room in Asser Levy Recreation Center
address: Multi-Use Room in Asser Levy Recreation Center

name: Strong and Steady Circuit Training
description: Strong & Steady Circuit Training is a full-body workout designed for high-energy fun. This class focuses on the mind-body connection, combining steady, controlled movements with high-energy sequences to build strength. Come ready to move, breathe, and leave feeling energized.

name: Zumba Gold®
description: Zumba Gold® offers a low-impact workout set to a fusion of Latin, International and popular music dance themes, creating a dynamic, exciting, effective fitness system! The routines feature aerobic fitness interval training with a combination of fast and slow rhythms that challenge both the heart and muscles. With fun music and easy-to-follow steps, you can't go wrong!
location_name: Ryan Health- Chelsea Clinton Community Health Center
address: Ryan Health- Chelsea Clinton Community Health Center

name: Uplifted Dance Fitness
description: Uplift your spirit with this dance fitness class! Come and enjoy an array of upbeat music featuring gospel. This class is a fusion of motivation, inspiration, fitness, and fun. Move your body, relax your mind, enjoy the community, and lift your spirit!
location_name: Multi-Use Room - 2nd Fl in Sorrentino Recreation Center
address: Multi-Use Room - 2nd Fl in Sorrentino Recreation Center

name: Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in this Yoga class with Ambeka, a class to be enjoyed by all. Experience a fun and light-hearted, mid-intensity flow that will move you seamlessly from Sun Salutation to a final peak pose. We focus on inversions and breath work to ensure you leave class feeling challenged and balanced.
address: Entrance - 215th Place and 33rd Road in Crocheron Park

name: BollyX
description: BollyX is a Bollywood inspired cardio dance fitness class inspired and is driven by the heart-pumping, energetic rhythms of cultural music from all parts of India. The class embodies dynamic choreography and easy-to-follow dance aerobics. Choreography and workout routines have been set so everyone can enjoy the class (modifications for individual steps can be provided in class). No dance experience or Bollywood familiarity is required - just come with comfortable shoes, water, and your smiles! Participants must sign in at the front desk and bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
location_name: Dance Room in Chelsea Recreation Center
address: Dance Room in Chelsea Recreation Center

name: Chair Yoga
description: This engaging chair yoga session blends gentle stretches, mindful breathing, and light strengthening exercises to leave you feeling relaxed yet revitalized. All movements are easy on the joints while enhancing flexibility, posture, and overall well-being. Ideal for beginners, older adults, or anyone looking for a supportive way to stay active.

name: RETRO Dance Fitness
description: Come dance to classic pop tunes: Top 40, Disco, Electronic and more! This class is 45 minutes of peppy beats to enhance your cardio conditioning. A low-impact and fun workout; it is open to any fitness level. Let's dance away blue Monday!
location_name: Rock Climbing Wall Room in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center
address: Rock Climbing Wall Room in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center

name: Harlem Run: Monday Night Run
description: Harlem Run brings free weekly and inclusive pacer-led runs and walks throughout Harlem. Join us for a different route through Harlem, beginning and ending at Marcus Garvey Park. Paces available include 7 min to 12 min, run\/walk, and walk groups. Open to all ages and abilities. Bag drop available, though not guaranteed.

name: Virtual Yoga
description: Welcome to this online yoga community in Vinyasa Yoga Flow with Rika. This class encompasses a warm-up, mindful flow, balance poses, gentle inversions, and final relaxation. Discover empowerment and affirmation to embrace who you are on and off your mat. Mats and props are recommended but not required. Leave feeling refreshed and centered, and knowing you have a safe community. The Zoom link will be sent out two hours before the class and again right before the class starts to registered participants.

name: Free Movie On The Waterfront: Ferris Bueller's Day Off
description: Join us for free movies on the waterfront! On the Great Lawn in Astoria Park. We will be showing Ferris Bueller's Day Off (Rated PG13)
address: Astoria Park Lawn in Astoria Park
